title: Mastering Minecraft Events
description: Deep dive into Paper/Spigot events with real examples, logic handling, edge cases, and testing.

lessons.title: Introduction to Events
lessons.content:
# What Are Events?

Events let your plugin react to things happening on the server.

## Examples
- Player joins
- Block breaks
- Player sends chat

## Basic Example
```java
@EventHandler
public void onJoin(PlayerJoinEvent event) {
    event.getPlayer().sendMessage("Welcome to the server!");
}
```

## Logic Breakdown
- Server fires event
- Your listener catches it
- Your code runs

## Common Mistakes
- Forgetting @EventHandler
- Not registering the listener
- Using wrong event type

quiz.question: What object gives access to the player?

lessons.title: Registering Event Listeners
lessons.content:
# Registering Events

## Step 1: Create Listener
```java
public class JoinListener implements Listener {
}
```

## Step 2: Register in Main Class
```java
getServer().getPluginManager().registerEvents(new JoinListener(), this);
```

## Logic
- Listener class exists
- Server must be told to use it
- Without this, nothing runs

## Test
Add a print message:
```java
getLogger().info("Listener loaded");
```
Check console when starting server.

lessons.title: Chat Events
Event Priority & Advanced Control
lessons.content:
# Event Priority

## Example
```java
@EventHandler(priority = EventPriority.HIGH)
public void onBreak(BlockBreakEvent event) {}
```

## Priorities
- LOW
- NORMAL
- HIGH
- HIGHEST
- MONITOR

## Logic
Higher priority = runs later

## Ignore Cancelled
```java
@EventHandler(ignoreCancelled = true)
```

Skips cancelled events.

lessons.title: Testing & Debugging Events
lessons.content:
# Debugging Events

## Print Logs
```java
getLogger().info("Event fired");
```

## Common Bugs
- Not registered
- Wrong event
- Plugin not reloaded

## Testing Strategy
1. Trigger event manually
2. Check console
3. Add debug logs

## Advanced Test
Use conditions:
```java
if(event.getPlayer().hasPermission("test")) {}
```
